root_text.an3.50:1.1: “Tīhi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato mahācoro sandhimpi chindati, nillopampi harati, ekāgārikampi karoti, paripanthepi tiṭṭhati.
root_text.an3.50:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, mahācoro visamanissito ca hoti, gahananissito ca hoti, balavanissito ca hoti.
root_text.an3.50:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, mahācoro nadīviduggaṁ vā nissito hoti pabbatavisamaṁ vā.
root_text.an3.50:1.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, mahācoro visamanissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, mahācoro tiṇagahanaṁ vā nissito hoti, rukkhagahanaṁ vā rodhaṁ vā mahāvanasaṇḍaṁ vā.
root_text.an3.50:2.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, mahācoro gahananissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, mahācoro rājānaṁ vā rājamahāmattānaṁ vā nissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:3.4: ‘sace maṁ koci kiñci vakkhati, ime me rājāno vā rājamahāmattā vā pariyodhāya atthaṁ bhaṇissantī’ti.
root_text.an3.50:3.5: Sace naṁ koci kiñci āha, tyāssa rājāno vā rājamahāmattā vā pariyodhāya atthaṁ bhaṇanti.
root_text.an3.50:3.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, mahācoro balavanissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:3.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tīhi aṅgehi samannāgato mahācoro sandhimpi chindati, nillopampi harati, ekāgārikampi karoti, paripanthepi tiṭṭhati.
root_text.an3.50:4.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tīhi aṅgehi samannāgato pāpabhikkhu khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an3.50:4.3: Idha, bhikkhave, pāpabhikkhu visamanissito ca hoti gahananissito ca balavanissito ca.
root_text.an3.50:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, pāpabhikkhu visamena kāyakammena samannāgato hoti, visamena vacīkammena samannāgato hoti, visamena manokammena samannāgato hoti.
root_text.an3.50:5.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pāpabhikkhu visamanissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, pāpabhikkhu micchādiṭṭhiko hoti, antaggāhikāya diṭṭhiyā samannāgato hoti.
root_text.an3.50:6.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pāpabhikkhu gahananissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, pāpabhikkhu rājānaṁ vā rājamahāmattānaṁ vā nissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:7.4: ‘sace maṁ koci kiñci vakkhati, ime me rājāno vā rājamahāmattā vā pariyodhāya atthaṁ bhaṇissantī’ti.
root_text.an3.50:7.5: Sace naṁ koci kiñci āha, tyāssa rājāno vā rājamahāmattā vā pariyodhāya atthaṁ bhaṇanti.
root_text.an3.50:7.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pāpabhikkhu balavanissito hoti.
root_text.an3.50:7.7: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato pāpabhikkhu khataṁ upahataṁ attānaṁ pariharati, sāvajjo ca hoti sānuvajjo ca viññūnaṁ, bahuñca apuññaṁ pasavatī”ti.

translation_text.an3.50:1.1: “Mendicants, a master thief with three factors breaks into houses, plunders wealth, steals from isolated buildings, and commits highway robbery.
translation_text.an3.50:1.3: A master thief relies on uneven ground, on thick cover, and on the powerful.
translation_text.an3.50:1.4: And how does a master thief rely on uneven ground?
translation_text.an3.50:1.5: It’s when a master thief relies on inaccessible riverlands or rugged mountains.
translation_text.an3.50:1.6: That’s how a master thief relies on uneven ground.
translation_text.an3.50:2.1: And how does a master thief rely on thick cover?
translation_text.an3.50:2.2: It’s when a master thief relies on thick grass, thick trees, a blind spot, or a large forest grove.
translation_text.an3.50:2.3: That’s how a master thief relies on thick cover.
translation_text.an3.50:3.1: And how does a master thief rely on the powerful?
translation_text.an3.50:3.2: It’s when a master thief relies on rulers or their chief ministers.
translation_text.an3.50:3.4: ‘If anyone accuses me of anything, these rulers or their chief ministers will speak in my defense in the case.’
translation_text.an3.50:3.6: That’s how a master thief relies on the powerful.
translation_text.an3.50:3.7: A master thief with these three factors breaks into houses, plunders wealth, steals from isolated buildings, and commits highway robbery.
translation_text.an3.50:4.1: In the same way, when a bad mendicant has three factors, they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.
translation_text.an3.50:4.3: A bad mendicant relies on uneven ground, on thick cover, and on the powerful.
translation_text.an3.50:5.1: And how does a bad mendicant rely on uneven ground?
translation_text.an3.50:5.2: It’s when a bad mendicant has unethical conduct by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.50:5.3: That’s how a bad mendicant relies on uneven ground.
translation_text.an3.50:6.1: And how does a bad mendicant rely on thick cover?
translation_text.an3.50:6.2: It’s when a bad mendicant has wrong view, he’s attached to an extremist view.
translation_text.an3.50:6.3: That’s how a bad mendicant relies on thick cover.
translation_text.an3.50:7.1: And how does a bad mendicant rely on the powerful?
translation_text.an3.50:7.2: It’s when a bad mendicant relies on rulers or their chief ministers.
translation_text.an3.50:7.4: ‘If anyone accuses me of anything, these rulers or their chief ministers will speak in my defense in the case.’
translation_text.an3.50:7.6: That’s how a bad mendicant relies on the powerful.
translation_text.an3.50:7.7: When a bad mendicant possesses these three qualities, they keep themselves broken and damaged. They deserve to be blamed and criticized by sensible people, and they brim with much wickedness.”
